title: Tactics: Overloading
summary: Make your opponent's piece do too much - then exploit it.
description: Overloading is when an enemy piece has too many defensive duties. By attacking one of its other obligations, you force it to give up defending something else, allowing you to capture material or deliver checkmate. This is a key GM-level tactical concept.
objectives: Identify pieces with multiple defensive duties
Learn to create double attacks on overloaded pieces
Understand how to force the overload
Master combining overload with other tactics
plans: Find pieces that defend multiple important squares or pieces
Attack one of their duties to force them to give up the other
Often a simple move can create an overload
commonMistakes: Don't overload if the piece can simply move away
Calculate whether the opponent can defend both duties
Sometimes you need to remove other defenders first
completionSummary: You've learned to identify and exploit overloaded enemy pieces.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Recapture
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.instruction: Black moves the bishop to b6.

steps.title: Capture on d6
steps.instruction: Capture on d6 with the pawn!
steps.coaching: This pawn attacks the bishop on b6 AND the queen on d8! The bishop is overloaded - it must defend both!
steps.hint: Capture on d6 with the pawn!

steps.title: Bishop captures
steps.instruction: The bishop must capture the pawn.
steps.coaching: The bishop captures. But now we can capture the bishop!

steps.title: Win the bishop
steps.instruction: Capture on f7! We've won a piece through overloading!
steps.hint: Capture the bishop on d6 with the bishop!
